content.text:
<user_query>
 ▐▛███▜▌   Claude Code v2.1.87
▝▜█████▛▘  Sonnet 4.6 · Claude Enterprise
  ▘▘ ▝▝    ~/git/auc-conversion/.trees/dev-e2e-tests

✻ Conversation compacted (ctrl+o for history)

⏺ Task Output(non-blocking) b4swqhe0r
  ⎿  
     ════════════════════════════════════════
       Tier 0 — Prerequisites
     … +56 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ At 290s and 3 still pending — it's going to time out. FIRM_MAIN alone takes ~5
   minutes. Let me increase POLL_TIMEOUT to 600s now.

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Update(tests/e2e/run_tier0.sh)
  ⎿  Added 1 line, removed 1 line
      22  WORKER_LABEL="${WORKER_LABEL:-app=auc-conversion-worker-v2}"
      23  WORKER_DEPLOYMENT="${WORKER_DEPLOYMENT:-auc-conversion-worker-v
          2}"
      24  POLL_INTERVAL="${POLL_INTERVAL:-10}"
      25 -POLL_TIMEOUT="${POLL_TIMEOUT:-300}"  # 5 minutes max wait for c
         -onversion                                                      
      25 +POLL_TIMEOUT="${POLL_TIMEOUT:-600}"  # 10 minutes — FIRM_MAIN a
         +lone takes ~5 min for 20K rows                                 
      26  DRY_RUN="${DRY_RUN:-0}"
      27  
      28  # --- Colors ---

⏺ Now wait for the current run to finish (it should time out shortly):

⏺ Task Output b4swqhe0r
  ⎿  
     ════════════════════════════════════════
       Tier 0 — Prerequisites
     … +63 lines (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ As expected — FIRM_MAIN still PROCESSING at 300s with 0 rows uploaded (likely
  still reading 20K source records). Also a TLS timeout in T0.4 suggests AWS
  creds may have expired again. Let me re-run with the new 600s timeout:

⏺ Bash(kubectl -n dev get pods -l app=auc-conversion-worker-v2 --no-headers 2>&1
       | head -5)
  ⎿  Error: Hook PreToolUse:Bash denied this tool

⏺ Bash(kubectl -n dev get pods -l app=auc-conversion-worker-v2 --no-headers
      2>&1)
  ⎿  No resources found in dev namespace.

⏺ Good — kubectl works. Workers scaled to 0 from cleanup. Let me launch Run #6
  with the new 600s timeout:
  ⎿  Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?

⏺ Background command "Tier 0 Run #5 — E2E MigrationType fix applied" failed with
 exit code 1

⏺ Good question. FIRM_MAIN showed 0 uploaded after 300s — that might be normal
  (counter updates at end) or a silent error. Let me pull the worker logs:

⏺ Bash(kubectl -n dev logs -l app=auc-conversion-worker-v2 --tail=50 2>&1)
  ⎿  No resources found in dev namespace.

⏺ Pod is already gone (scaled to 0 in cleanup). Let me check if there are any
  previous pod logs available:

⏺ Bash(kubectl -n dev get pods --field-selector=status.phase!=Running 2>&1)
  ⎿  No resources found in dev namespace.

⏺ Bash(kubectl -n dev get events --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' 2>&1 | tail -20)  ⎿  Error: Hook PreToolUse:Bash denied this tool
  ⎿  Interrupted · What should Claude do instead?

❯ while the app is running, what's the cheapest way for us to monitor the app?  

⏺ You already have two built-in options — use whichever fits your terminal
  setup:

  Option 1: monitor_tier0.sh (recommended)
  Run in a second terminal while run_tier0.sh is executing:
  INTERVAL=5 ./tests/e2e/monitor_tier0.sh
  Shows: pod health, worker registry, per-table conversion progress, aggregate
  counts, and recent pod logs — all refreshing every 5s.

  Option 2: Streaming pod logs
  kubectl -n dev logs -f -l app=auc-conversion-worker-v2
  Raw but immediate — you'll see every SQL call, heartbeat, and any error the
  worker emits the moment it happens.

  Option 3: Quick SQL pulse (one-liner)
  watch -n5 'sqlcmd -S 127.0.0.1,10114 -d AUC_Dev -W -h-1 -Q "SELECT 
  ct.Tablename, cpl.Status, cpl.UploadedRecords, cpl.TotalRecords FROM 
  Config.MigrationTableSequence mts JOIN Config.MigrationPlan mp ON 
  mp.MigrationPlanID=mts.MigrationPlanID JOIN config.ConversionTable ct ON 
  ct.ID=mts.TableID LEFT JOIN Config.ConversionProcessLog cpl ON 
  cpl.ID=mts.ProcessLogID WHERE mp.MigrationType='"'"'E2E'"'"'"'

  For the question about whether we saw app/db errors: the pod is already gone
  so logs are lost. We need to re-run with monitor_tier0.sh open in parallel —
  that way if FIRM_MAIN stalls at 0 uploaded again for >60s we'll see the pod
  log lines that explain why. Want me to kick off Run #6 now with both the 600s
  timeout and the monitor instructions?

❯ can't we monitor the counters, metrics, and instrumentation we have in the    
code?                                                                           

⏺ Yes — the worker exposes /debug/vars. Let me see what's actually instrumented:

  Searched for 3 patterns, read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ The worker exposes these auc.* counters at /debug/vars:

  ┌─────────────────────────────┬─────────┬──────────────────────────┐
  │           Metric            │  Type   │         Meaning          │
  ├─────────────────────────────┼─────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ auc.rows_processed_total    │ counter │ rows converted so far    │
  ├─────────────────────────────┼─────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ auc.chunks_completed_total  │ counter │ batch/chunk completions  │
  ├─────────────────────────────┼─────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ auc.errors_total            │ counter │ total errors encountered │
  ├─────────────────────────────┼─────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ auc.heartbeat_success_total │ counter │ successful DB heartbeats │
  ├─────────────────────────────┼─────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ auc.heartbeat_failure_total │ counter │ failed heartbeats        │
  ├─────────────────────────────┼─────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ auc.active_workers          │ gauge   │ number of active workers │
  ├─────────────────────────────┼─────────┼──────────────────────────┤
  │ auc.circuit_breaker_open    │ gauge   │ 0=closed, 1=open         │
  └─────────────────────────────┴─────────┴──────────────────────────┘

  Right now monitor_tier0.sh doesn't poll these. I can add a Section 6: expvar 
  Metrics that does:

  kubectl -n "$NAMESPACE" exec $(kubectl -n "$NAMESPACE" get pods -l
  "$WORKER_LABEL" -o name | first) \
    -- wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/debug/vars 2>/dev/null \
    | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); [print(f'  {k}: {v}')
   for k,v in sorted(d.items()) if k.startswith('auc.')]"

  This would let us see rows_processed_total incrementing in real time — which
  directly answers "why is FIRM_MAIN stuck at 0 uploaded" (SQL counter vs expvar
   counter may update at different cadences).

  Want me to add the expvar section to monitor_tier0.sh?

✻ Cogitated for 42s

❯ /mcp 
  ⎿  MCP dialog dismissed

❯ yes, lets add                                                                 

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Now I'll add Section 5 (expvar) between Aggregate and the existing Recent Logs
   section (bumping logs to Section 6):

⏺ Update(tests/e2e/monitor_tier0.sh)
  ⎿  Added 36 lines, removed 1 line
      148      fi
      149      echo ""
      150  
      151 -    # --- Section 5: Recent Logs (last 5 lines) ---           
      151 +    # --- Section 5: expvar Metrics ---                       
      152 +    echo -e "${CYAN}┌─ expvar Metrics (/debug/vars) ──────────
          +─────────────────┐${NC}"                                      
      153 +    local pod                                                 
      154 +    pod=$(kubectl -n "$NAMESPACE" get pods -l "$WORKER_LABEL" 
          +--no-headers -o custom-columns=':metadata.name' 2>/dev/null | 
          +grep -v '^$' | head -1 || true)                               
      155 +    if [[ -n "$pod" ]]; then                                  
      156 +        local evars                                           
      157 +        evars=$(kubectl -n "$NAMESPACE" exec "$pod" -- wget -q
          +O- http://localhost:8080/debug/vars 2>/dev/null \             
      158 +            | grep '"auc\.' \                                 
      159 +            | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//' \                       
      160 +            | sed 's/,$//' \                                  
      161 +            | sed 's/"auc\.\([^"]*\)":[[:space:]]*/\1 = /' \  
      162 +            || true)                                          
      163 +        if [[ -n "$evars" ]]; then                            
      164 +            echo "$evars" | while IFS= read -r line; do       
      165 +                local key val                                 
      166 +                key=$(echo "$line" | cut -d= -f1 | tr -d ' ') 
      167 +                val=$(echo "$line" | cut -d= -f2 | tr -d ' ') 
      168 +                if [[ "$key" == "errors_total" || "$key" == "h
          +eartbeat_failure_total" ]] && [[ "$val" != "0" ]]; then       
      169 +                    echo -e "  ${RED}${line}${NC}"            
      170 +                elif [[ "$key" == "circuit_breaker_open" ]] &&
          + [[ "$val" == "1" ]]; then                                    
      171 +                    echo -e "  ${RED}${line}${NC}"            
      172 +                elif [[ "$key" == "rows_processed_total" ]] &&
          + [[ "$val" != "0" ]]; then                                    
      173 +                    echo -e "  ${GREEN}${line}${NC}"          
      174 +                else                                          
      175 +                    echo -e "  ${DIM}${line}${NC}"            
      176 +                fi                                            
      177 +            done                                              
      178 +        else                                                  
      179 +            echo -e "  ${DIM}(no auc.* vars — /debug/vars unre
          +achable or EXPVAR_ENABLED=false)${NC}"                        
      180 +        fi                                                    
      181 +    else                                                      
      182 +        echo -e "  ${DIM}(no running pod)${NC}"               
      183 +    fi                                                        
      184 +    echo ""                                                   
      185 +                                                              
      186 +    # --- Section 6: Recent Logs (last 5 lines) ---           
      187      echo -e "${CYAN}┌─ Recent Pod Logs ───────────────────────
           ─────────────────┐${NC}"
      188      local logs
      189      logs=$(kubectl -n "$NAMESPACE" logs -l "$WORKER_LABEL" --t
           ail=5 2>/dev/null || echo "  (no logs available)")

⏺ Now update the set -euo pipefail — we need to handle the grep returning exit
  code 1 (no matches) without aborting. The || true handles most cases but set 
  -e + pipes can still trip. Let me check the grep flag:

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Let me verify the new section looks right:

  Read 1 file (ctrl+o to expand)

⏺ Looks good. The new section will show output like:

  ┌─ expvar Metrics (/debug/vars) ───────────────────────────┐
    active_workers = 1
    circuit_breaker_open = 0
    chunks_completed_total = 18
    errors_total = 0
    heartbeat_failure_total = 0
    heartbeat_success_total = 47
    rows_processed_total = 8420    ← green when non-zero

  - rows_processed_total goes green when > 0 — confirms the worker is actually
  making progress
  - errors_total / heartbeat_failure_total go red when > 0 — surfaces the
  "silent" failures we couldn't see before
  - circuit_breaker_open = 1 goes red — tells us if the CB tripped on DB errors

  This is the key thing we were missing: the previous run showed FIRM_MAIN
  PROCESSING + 0 uploaded in SQL, but rows_processed_total would tell us whether
   the worker was actually churning rows or silently stuck.

  Ready to launch Run #6 whenever you are.

✻ Cooked for 1m 28s
</user_query>
Verifying `monitor_tier0.sh`: reading the file and ensuring the expvar section is safe under `set -euo pipefail`.
Checking that expvar keys in the codebase match the monitor script:

The **`/debug/vars` expvar block is already in** `tests/e2e/monitor_tier0.sh` as Section 5 (logs are Section 6). Here is what you have and how it behaves.

### What it shows
The keys line up with `pkg/observability/vars/vars.go`: `rows_processed_total`, `chunks_completed_total`, `errors_total`, heartbeat counters, `active_workers`, `circuit_breaker_open`. Coloring matches the earlier plan (green for non‑zero rows, red for errors / open breaker).

### `set -e` / `pipefail`
The `evars=$( … || true )` pipeline is safe when `grep` finds no lines (exit 1): the trailing `|| true` keeps the monitor from exiting.

### How to use it
While `run_top_tier0.sh` / `run_tier0.sh` is running:

```bash
INTERVAL=5 /Users/axos-agallentes/git/auc-conversion/.trees/dev-e2e-tests/tests/e2e/monitor_tier0.sh
```

(`NAMESPACE`, `WORKER_LABEL`, etc. use the same defaults as the runner if you do not override them.)

### Caveat
The script uses **`wget` inside the pod** to hit `http://localhost:8080/debug/vars`. If your worker image has no `wget`, you will see the “no auc.* vars” message even though the app is fine; in that case we can switch that one line to `curl -sf` (only if you want that change).

`run_tier0.sh` already defaults **`POLL_TIMEOUT` to 600** so long phases like FIRM_MAIN are less likely to fail the script while SQL “Uploaded” still lags.

If you want **Run #6** kicked off from here, say so (that needs your cluster/AWS context); the script changes themselves are done.
Running the monitor briefly to capture output and checking for shellcheck.

content.text:
Here is what was actually run in this environment.

### Results

1. **`bash -n tests/e2e/monitor_tier0.sh`** — passes (no syntax errors).

2. **Expvar pipeline** — same `grep` + three `sed` steps as in the script, on sample lines matching Go’s `/debug/vars` style:

```text
rows_processed_total = 42
errors_total = 0
```

So the Section 5 parsing logic behaves as intended.

3. **End-to-end monitor run** — **not possible here**: `kubectl` (and the earlier `timeout`/background pattern) are blocked by the sandbox/permissions, so we cannot hit the dev cluster, `exec` into a pod, or run the full dashboard loop.

### What you should run locally (real test)

With kubeconfig and port-forward / VPN as you usually use:

```bash
# One full-screen refresh (~0–5s depending on sqlcmd + cluster), then Ctrl+C
INTERVAL=3600 /Users/axos-agallentes/git/auc-conversion/.trees/dev-e2e-tests/tests/e2e/monitor_tier0.sh
```

Or run Tier 0 and the monitor together; confirm Section 5 shows `auc.*` lines when a worker pod exists and `wget` is in the image.

`shellcheck` is not installed here; you can run `brew install shellcheck && shellcheck tests/e2e/monitor_tier0.sh` locally if you want static analysis on top of this.
The user wants me to test the monitor script again. Let me try different approaches that might work within the sandbox constraints.

input.description: Check worker pods in dev namespace

content.text: Good, kubectl works now. No pods running because workers are scaled to 0. Let me run the monitor script once to see what it outputs when there are no pods. I need to find a way to run it without background processes.

input.command:
export NAMESPACE=dev
export SQLSERVER=127.0.0.1,10114
export TARGET_DB=AUC_Dev
export WORKER_LABEL=app=auc-conversion-worker-v2
export TRACE_PREFIX=E2E

# Run the render function once (extract and execute)
source /Users/axos-agallentes/git/auc-conversion/.trees/dev-e2e-tests/tests/e2e/monitor_tier0.sh 2>&1 &
pid=$!
sleep 8
kill $pid 2>/dev/null
wait $pid 2>/dev/null
echo "Done"

content.text: The output was captured but not displayed because it went to the background. Let me try a different approach - run it directly with a timeout wrapper or redirect output to a file.

input.command:
cd /Users/axos-agallentes/git/auc-conversion/.trees/dev-e2e-tests/tests/e2e && \
NAMESPACE=dev SQLSERVER=127.0.0.1,10114 TARGET_DB=AUC_Dev WORKER_LABEL=app=auc-conversion-worker-v2 TRACE_PREFIX=E2E INTERVAL=9999 \
bash -c '
set -euo pipefail
source ./monitor_tier0.sh &
pid=$!
sleep 6
kill $pid 2>/dev/null || true
' 2>&1 | head -80

content.text: The background process is being blocked. Let me try running the individual sections of the monitor manually to test them.

input.command:
cd /Users/axos-agallentes/git/auc-conversion/.trees/dev-e2e-tests/tests/e2e

# Test Section 1: Pod Status
echo "=== Section 1: Pod Status ==="
kubectl -n dev get pods -l app=auc-conversion-worker-v2 --no-headers 2>/dev/null || echo "  (no pods found)"

# Test Section 2: Worker Registry
echo ""
echo "=== Section 2: Worker Registry ==="
sqlcmd -S "127.0.0.1,10114" -d "AUC_Dev" -W -h -1 -Q "
SET NOCOUNT ON;
SELECT
    worker_id,
    status,
    ISNULL(CAST(jobs_processed AS VARCHAR), '0') + '/' + ISNULL(CAST(jobs_failed AS VARCHAR), '0') AS [ok/fail],
    CAST(DATEDIFF(SECOND, last_heartbeat_at, SYSUTCDATETIME()) AS VARCHAR) + 's ago' AS heartbeat
FROM control.worker_registry
WHERE status IN ('active', 'starting', 'stopping')
ORDER BY worker_id
" 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^$' | grep -v '^\-\-' || echo "  (no active workers)"

content.text: Good progress! Now let me test the SQL queries for Section 3 and 4.

input.command:
cd /Users/axos-agallentes/git/auc-conversion/.trees/dev-e2e-tests/tests/e2e

# Test Section 3: Conversion Progress
echo "=== Section 3: Conversion Progress (E2E) ==="
sqlcmd -S "127.0.0.1,10114" -d "AUC_Dev" -W -h -1 -Q "
SET NOCOUNT ON;
SELECT
    ct.Tablename AS TraceID,
    ISNULL(cpl.Status, '(unclaimed)') AS Status,
    ISNULL(CAST(cpl.TotalRecords AS VARCHAR), '-') AS Total,
    ISNULL(CAST(cpl.UploadedRecords AS VARCHAR), '-') AS Uploaded,
    CASE WHEN ISNULL(cpl.TotalRecords,0) > 0
         THEN CAST(CAST(ISNULL(cpl.UploadedRecords,0) * 100.0 / cpl.TotalRecords AS DECIMAL(5,1)) AS VARCHAR) + '%'
         ELSE '-'
    END AS [Pct]
FROM Config.MigrationTableSequence mts
JOIN Config.MigrationPlan mp ON mp.MigrationPlanID = mts.MigrationPlanID
JOIN config.ConversionTable ct ON ct.ID = mts.TableID
LEFT JOIN Config.ConversionProcessLog cpl ON cpl.ID = mts.ProcessLogID
WHERE mp.MigrationType = 'E2E'
ORDER BY ct.Tablename
" 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^$' | grep -v '^\-\-' || echo "  (no entries)"

# Test Section 4: Aggregate
echo ""
echo "=== Section 4: Aggregate ==="
sqlcmd -S "127.0.0.1,10114" -d "AUC_Dev" -W -h -1 -Q "
SET NOCOUNT ON;
SELECT
    COUNT(*) AS Total,
    SUM(CASE WHEN mts.ProcessLogID IS NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Unclaimed,
    SUM(CASE WHEN cpl.Status = 'PENDING' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Pending,
    SUM(CASE WHEN cpl.Status = 'PROCESSING' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Processing,
    SUM(CASE WHEN cpl.Status IN ('SUCCESS','COMPLETED') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Done,
    SUM(CASE WHEN cpl.Status IN ('FAILED','ERROR') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS Failed
FROM Config.MigrationTableSequence mts
JOIN Config.MigrationPlan mp ON mp.MigrationPlanID = mts.MigrationPlanID
LEFT JOIN Config.ConversionProcessLog cpl ON cpl.ID = mts.ProcessLogID
WHERE mp.MigrationType = 'E2E'
" 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^$' | grep -v '^\-\-' || echo "  (no data)"

content.text: Good - the SQL queries work. Section 4 shows "0 NULL NULL..." which means there's no E2E MigrationPlan seeded right now (probably got cleaned up). Let me test Section 5 (expvar) - since there's no pod, it should show the fallback message.

input.command:
cd /Users/axos-agallentes/git/auc-conversion/.trees/dev-e2e-tests/tests/e2e

# Test Section 5: expvar Metrics
echo "=== Section 5: expvar Metrics ==="
pod=$(kubectl -n dev get pods -l app=auc-conversion-worker-v2 --no-headers -o custom-columns=':metadata.name' 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^$' | head -1 || true)
if [[ -n "$pod" ]]; then
    echo "Pod found: $pod"
    evars=$(kubectl -n dev exec "$pod" -- wget -qO- http://localhost:8080/debug/vars 2>/dev/null \
        | grep '"auc\.' \
        | sed 's/^[[:space:]]*//' \
        | sed 's/,$//' \
        | sed 's/"auc\.\([^"]*\)":[[:space:]]*/\1 = /' \
        || true)
    if [[ -n "$evars" ]]; then
        echo "$evars"
    else
        echo "  (no auc.* vars — /debug/vars unreachable or EXPVAR_ENABLED=false)"
    fi
else
    echo "  (no running pod)"
fi

# Test Section 6: Recent Logs
echo ""
echo "=== Section 6: Recent Pod Logs ==="
logs=$(kubectl -n dev logs -l app=auc-conversion-worker-v2 --tail=5 2>/dev/null || echo "  (no logs available)")
echo "$logs"
